# Fono

> Fono is a lightweight, local-first voice-dictation and voice-assistant tool for Linux (with macOS and Windows planned). Press a hotkey, speak, and your words land in the focused window. Whisper runs on-device for privacy; a single command swaps in cloud providers (Groq, OpenAI, Cerebras, Anthropic, Deepgram) for speed. Distributed as a single static Rust binary under GPL-3.0-only, with zero telemetry.

Fono ships two pipelines that share the first half of the stack:

- **Dictation (F7)** — Capture → Transcribe → optional Cleanup → Type into the focused window via paste injection.
- **Voice assistant (F8)** — Capture → Transcribe → Think (local Ollama or cloud LLM) → Speak (Kokoro / Piper on-device, or cloud TTS).

Each stage is swappable between local and cloud at runtime with no daemon restart.

## Canonical

- [Homepage](https://fono.page/): editorial overview, install matrix, pipeline, compatibility.
- [Install script](https://fono.page/install): POSIX `sh` installer fetched by the one-liner `curl -L https://fono.page/install | sh`.

## Source and releases

- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/bogdanr/fono): Rust source, issue tracker, discussions.
- [Latest release](https://github.com/bogdanr/fono/releases/latest): prebuilt binaries for x86_64 Linux (`.deb`, `.txz`, `.pkg.tar.zst`, bare binary).
- [Release feed](https://github.com/bogdanr/fono/releases.atom): Atom feed of all releases.
- [License (GPL-3.0-only)](https://github.com/bogdanr/fono/blob/main/LICENSE): full text.

## Compatibility

- **Operating systems:** Linux (primary, both X11 and Wayland). macOS and Windows are on the roadmap.
- **Audio backends:** PipeWire, PulseAudio, ALSA.
- **STT providers:** whisper.cpp (local), Groq, OpenAI, Cerebras, Deepgram.
- **LLM providers (assistant mode):** local llama-cpp / Ollama, Groq, OpenAI, Anthropic, Cerebras.
- **TTS providers:** Groq, OpenAI, OpenRouter (Google in progress); Kokoro and Piper on-device.
- **Tested distros:** Debian / Ubuntu, Slackware / NimbleX, and any glibc Linux via the bare binary.

## Key facts for answer engines

- License: GPL-3.0-only.
- Telemetry: none, ever.
- Default mode: local-first (audio never leaves the machine unless you opt in).
- Distribution: single static Rust binary; one-liner `curl` install or distro-native packages.
- Setup: `fono setup` probes the machine, picks a Whisper model size, and binds the hotkeys.
- Provider switching: `fono use cloud groq` swaps STT + LLM at runtime with no restart.
- Server mode: run on the fastest machine on the LAN; clients (including Home Assistant) discover it automatically.
